name: Minamoto no Yoshitsune
description: Minamoto no Yoshitsune (1159–1189) was a prominent military commander of the Minamoto clan during the late Heian and early Kamakura periods of Japan. Born Ushiwakamaru, his early life was marked by tragedy and exile following his father's defeat in the Heiji Rebellion. He was raised in Kurama Temple, where he learned martial arts and strategy, developing extraordinary combat skills. Yoshitsune rose to prominence during the Genpei War, leading the Minamoto forces to victory against the Taira clan. His tactical genius was evident in battles like Ichi-no-Tani and Dan-no-ura, where he employed innovative strategies to overcome seemingly insurmountable odds. Despite his crucial contributions to the Minamoto victory, Yoshitsune later fell out of favor with his elder brother, Minamoto no Yoritomo, the founder of the Kamakura shogunate. Yoritomo, fueled by paranoia and political ambition, viewed Yoshitsune as a threat to his authority. This led to a bitter conflict between the brothers, culminating in Yoshitsune's exile and eventual suicide. Yoshitsune's life is a tapestry of triumph and tragedy, making him a legendary figure in Japanese history and folklore. He is remembered as a brilliant military strategist, a skilled warrior, and a tragic hero betrayed by his own kin. His story continues to inspire and captivate, embodying themes of loyalty, courage, and the fleeting nature of power. He remains a popular subject in literature, theater, and art.
subtitle: The tragic hero of the Genpei War

keyMyths.title: Training at Kurama Temple
keyMyths.description: Yoshitsune trained in martial arts at Kurama Temple, learning from tengu (mountain goblins) or skilled monks, depending on the version. This training shaped him into an exceptional warrior, preparing him for his future battles.

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Ichi-no-Tani
keyMyths.description: Yoshitsune led a daring raid down a steep cliff to outflank the Taira forces at Ichi-no-Tani. This audacious maneuver secured a crucial victory for the Minamoto clan.

keyMyths.title: Yoshitsune's Final Stand
keyMyths.description: Cornered by Yoritomo's forces, Yoshitsune committed suicide at Koromogawa. His loyal retainer, Benkei, died standing guard outside, a symbol of unwavering loyalty.
